content:
Character: Leonidas Argyros
Perception: Leonidas is tall, dark, sculpted like a Praxiteles bronze — the kind of young man who'd look at home on an Attic vase. He carries his father's death like an anchor around his ankle, though he'd never say so. His knowledge of ancient Greece is formidable and his writing, when he lets me read it, is beautiful. Passionate but too controlled; he needs to let something crack. I see him as a protégé of sorts — not in my field, but in the broader discipline of caring deeply about something the world considers useless. I know his fellowship is ending. I know he keeps an olive tree alive on his windowsill. I suspect he's writing something he hasn't told his committee about.
